---
title: "RVAT NLQ Interface Project Structure"
author: "Diógenes Cruz de Arcelino"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  # HTML output (primary deliverable)
  html_document:
    toc: true            # enable table of contents
    toc_depth: 3         # include up to level-3 headers
    theme: cosmo         # Bootstrap theme for styling
    highlight: tango     # syntax highlighting style

  # Word output (for stakeholders / reporting)
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3

  # PDF output (for archival / formal docs)
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    
---

<style>
.box {
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 15px;
  margin-bottom: 15px;
  background-color: #f9f9f9;
}

.tree {
  font-family: "Courier New", monospace;
  white-space: pre;
  background-color: #f4f6f8;

  padding: 15px;
  border-radius: 10px;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;

  font-size: 0.95em;
  line-height: 1.4;

  overflow-x: auto;   /* Enables horizontal scrolling */
  display: block;
}

/* Must be OUTSIDE .tree */
.tree::-webkit-scrollbar {
  height: 8px;
}

.tree::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
  background: #bbb;
  border-radius: 4px;
}
</style>


# 📘 Overview

<div class="box">
This repository is organized to support a reproducible, maintainable, and scalable Natural Language Query (NLQ) interface for RVAT data. The structure separates core logic, application code, data pipelines, and analysis.
</div>

# 🗂️ Project Structure

<div class="tree"><pre>
rvat-nlq-interface/
└── rvat-nlq-interface/
    │
    ├── README.md
    ├── LICENSE
    ├── .gitignore
    ├── renv.lock                        # reproducibility
    ├── .Rprofile                        # auto-load renv
    │
    ├── R/
    │   ├── 01_db_connection.R           # database connection
    │   ├── 02_ollama_config.R           # Ollama + system prompt
    │   ├── 03_sql_pipeline.R            # NL → SQL → DB → result
    │   ├── 04_logging.R                 # logging + timing (VES)
    │   ├── 05_shiny_ui.R                # Shiny UI
    │   └── 06_shiny_server.R            # Shiny server
    │
    ├── app/
    │   ├── rvat_nlq_app.R               # entry point (Shiny wrapper)
    │   └── greeting.md                  # UI onboarding content /exteded greeting/ instructions
    │
    ├── scripts/
    │   ├── 00_renv_setup.R              # install/load dependencies
    |   ├── 10_data_preparation.R        # sets full rvat-db or varInfo_synthetic 
    |   ├── 20_run_triage_pipeline.R     # script that loops and dumps the files into data/triage/
    │   ├── 30_run_app.R                 # developer convenience script
    │   ├── 40_process_logs.R            # clean + transform logs
    │   └── 50_evaluate.R                # EM / EX / VES evaluation
    │
    ├── data/
    │   ├── triage/                            # Stored raw/processed triage runs here
    │   |    ├── triage_raw_metrics.csv        # the direct data table with eval_count, durations, etc.
    │   |    └── triage_decision_matrix.csv    # Simple 2-column table: [model_name, status (PASS/FAIL)]
    |   ├── raw/                               # log_&lt;model&gt;_&lt;date&gt;.rds
    │   ├── processed/                         # converted to .csv for data/evaluation and analysis/ 
    │   └── evaluation/                 
    │       └── golden_dataset.csv             # Benchmark NLQ/SQL queries/answers to compare with LLM's output 
    │
    ├── analysis/
    │   ├── 00_triage_filter.Rmd               #Generates the pass/fail triage chapter & charts
    |   ├── 01_exploratory.Rmd
    │   ├── 02_evaluation.Rmd
    │   └── figures/
    |       ├── triage_latency_comparison.png  #Visual distribution of tokens/sec vs duration
    |       └── evaluation_accuracy.png
    │
    ├── tests/
    │   └── checks_troubleshooting_scripts.R      #Includes functional checks used during development
    │
    ├── docs/
    │   ├── methodology.md
    │   └── architecture.md
</pre></div>



# 🔍 Components

<details open>
<summary><b>⚙️ Core Logic (R/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- Database connection (`01_db_connection.R`)
- Ollama + system prompt (`02_ollama_config.R`)
- Pipeline NL → SQL → DB → result (`03_sql_pipeline.R`)
- Output logging + timing (VES) (`04_logging.R`)
- Shiny UI (`05_shiny_ui.R`)
- Shiny server (`06_shiny_server.R`)
</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><b>🖥️ Application (app/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- `rvat_nlq_app.R`: Shiny-based NL → SQL interface         
- `greeting.md`: UI onboarding content  

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><b>🔄 Pipelines (scripts/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- `10_setup.R`: Environment setup  
- `20_run_app.R`: Developer convenience script  
- `30_process_logs.R`: Log processing (clean + transform logs) 
- `40_evaluate.R`: Evaluation pipeline (EM / EX / VES evaluation)  

**Exact Match (EM):**
Measured as whether the generated SQL query exactly matches the predefined golden (reference) SQL query.

**Execution Match (EX):**  
Measured as whether the result of executing the generated SQL query is identical to the result of executing the corresponding golden SQL query.

**Latency (VES):**  
Measured as total execution time from user query submission to database result return.

</div>
</details>


<details>
<summary><b>💾 Data Layer (data/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

**Structure:**

- `raw/` — raw outputs (`log_<model>_<date>.rds`)
- `processed/` — cleaned and exported data (.csv)
- `evaluation/` — golden dataset and evaluation outputs

**Experiment Tracking:**

Each experimental run is identified by:

- `model` — the LLM used (e.g., qwen2.5-coder)
- `run_id` — unique identifier per run

Logs are saved per run to enable reproducibility and model comparison.

</div>
</details>


<details>
<summary><b>📊 Analysis (analysis/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- Contains exploratory and evaluation reports
- Operates on processed logs (.csv derived from .rds)
- Produces visualisations and summaries for:
  - Exact Match (EM)
  - Execution Match (EX)
  - Latency (VES)

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><b>✅ Testing (tests/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- Verifies NL → SQL → DB pipeline behavior
- Includes functional checks used during development (demo validation steps)


**Example test (future):**

```r
testthat::expect_true(
  grepl("SELECT", generate_sql_ollama("show rows"))
)

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><b>📚 Documentation (docs/)</b></summary>

<div class="box">

- Methodology  
- Architecture design  

</div>
</details>

# 🔁 Reproducibility

<div class="box">

Restore the environment:

```r
renv::restore()
